name: Okra Seafood Gumbo
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil the crabs in lightly salted water for 20 minutes. Remove and set aside, reserving the cooking liquid.
2. In a non-cast iron skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of oil and 1 tablespoon of butter. Fry the okra until all traces of sliminess disappear.
3. In a large pot, melt remaining oil over medium heat. Stir in flour to make a dark brown roux. Cook for about 10 minutes, stirring constantly.
4. Add chopped onions, garlic, green peppers, and celery to the roux. Cook for an additional 5 minutes.
5. Add diced ham and cook for another 10 minutes on low heat.
6. Stir in tomatoes and shrimp, then simmer.
7. Boil the shrimp heads, peelings, and shells from the crabs in 8 cups of water for 15 minutes. Strain the liquid into the pot.
8. Add the reserved okra to the pot and stir gently. Cook for a few more minutes.
9. Add the remaining seasonings and simmer slowly for 2 hours.
10. Remove the backs, belly flaps, legs, and claws from the crabs. Clean out any unwanted material from the crabs, then break them into halves and lightly crack the claws.
11. Return the crab halves and cracked claws to the pot. Cook for an additional 15 minutes, adding chopped green onions during the last 5 minutes of cooking.
12. Serve over rice. If desired, sprinkle file over the rice before adding the gumbo.
